analysis_result: RAF_UP.V1_UP (MSigDB C6) in MPNST-G1 vs MPNST-G2: NES = -0.7799, FDR q-val = 0.9706 (nominal p = 0.9242). Negative NES indicates the pathway is enriched in MPNST-G2 (not statistically significant).
denominator_used: N = 12 samples; 6 MPNST_G1 (M1014, M2252, M2346, M2372, M3048, M803) vs 6 MPNST_G2 (M1677, M2325, M2377, M2491, M4719, M9413) from GSE207400_Matrix.txt.gz; ranked gene list of 14,992 genes (from 14,995 expressed, dropna across samples).
assumptions_made: Used gseapy.prerank with seed=42 and 1000 permutations; ranked metric was Welch's t-statistic (G1-vs-G2) on the provided log-normalised bulk RNA-seq matrix; gene set from 'MSigDB_Oncogenic_Signatures' (Enrichr/MSigDB C6) fetched via gseapy.get_library; 'MPNST-G1 vs MPNST-G2' interpreted as G1 as numerator (positive NES => up in G1); min_size=5, max_size=2000; samples matched by Tumor_ID between Clinical Data sheet (Methylation_Group column) and GSE207400 matrix columns; 2 MPNST_G1 (M3398, M6712) and 2 MPNST_G2 (M1933, M9450) listed in clinical sheet had no RNA-Seq column data.
step_by_step_analysis:
A. DATA LOADED
- GSE207400_Matrix.txt.gz: 14,995 genes x 48 samples (log-normalised bulk RNA-seq).
- 41467_2023_38432_MOESM4_ESM.xlsx (Clinical Data sheet): 108 rows, 15 columns; used Tumor_ID and Methylation_Group.

B. COHORT DEFINITION
- MPNST_G1: 6 samples present in RNA matrix (M1014, M2252, M2346, M2372, M3048, M803)
- MPNST_G2: 6 samples present in RNA matrix (M1677, M2325, M2377, M2491, M4719, M9413)
- Excluded (no RNA): M3398, M6712 (G1); M1933, M9450 (G2).

C. VARIABLE CODING
- Expression values: log-normalised values as provided.
- Ranked metric: Welch's t-statistic (scipy.stats.ttest_ind, equal_var=False), directed so positive values = up in MPNST_G1 vs MPNST_G2.
- Gene universe: 14,992 genes after dropping rows with NaN across the 12 samples and dropping duplicated gene symbols.

D. COMPUTATION
- gseapy.prerank(rnk=ranked_gene_list, gene_sets='MSigDB_Oncogenic_Signatures', permutation_num=1000, seed=42, min_size=5, max_size=2000).
- MSigDB_Oncogenic_Signatures contains 189 gene sets including 'RAF UP.V1 UP' and 'RAF UP.V1 DN'.
- Target pathway: 'RAF UP.V1 UP' (174 genes, 19 leading edge overlap out of 174).

E. RESULT
- RAF UP.V1 UP:
    ES = -0.1764
    NES = -0.7799
    Nominal p-val = 0.9242
    FDR q-val = 0.9706
    FWER p-val = 1.0
    Leading edge (19 genes): KAT2B; SLC31A2; ARHGAP26; ITGA6; CLMN; L1CAM; KCNN4; CORO1A; ABHD6; VTN; HEG1; PKD2; PSMB9; ERAP1; PYGL; ME2; PRKCH; ABCC3; BLNK.
- Interpretation: RAF_UP.V1_UP signature is not significantly enriched in either MPNST-G1 or MPNST-G2; the (slightly negative) NES points toward modest enrichment in MPNST-G2 rather than MPNST-G1, but is far from statistical significance (FDR ~0.97).

F. CAVEATS
- Very small sample sizes (n=6 per group) limit power and stability of prerank results; NES and FDR values are sensitive to ranking metric choice.
- The paper's Supp Fig 5c may use a different ranking metric (e.g., limma moderated t, signal-to-noise, or log2FC) and/or different MSigDB version; gseapy here pulls the Enrichr-mirrored MSigDB_Oncogenic_Signatures (gene set size 174) which may differ in membership from the MSigDB release used in the paper.
- 4 of the 16 MPNST tumors (2 in G1, 2 in G2) lack RNA-Seq data and are excluded.
- No batch correction applied; assumes matrix is already normalised as described.
